description: A place where water flows naturally from the ground. 'Thula' names where earth gives water—the emergence points where underground flows surface, offering drink to all who find them.
sound_symbolism: The word emerges from depth. 'Thu' /θu/ begins with soft earth-breath, water rising. Then 'la' releases in liquid flow, the spring pouring forth.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo thula' (the spring). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted. Distinguished from the season (different word). Sacred in many traditions as gift-points where earth offers water.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Springs were sacred sources—places where earth offered pure water freely, often honored with shrines and careful protection.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Springs represent natural water infrastructure—earth's own distribution system, models for understanding watershed and source protection.
